title: FoundrySpark sponsorship: provisional credits & milestone top-up approval
preview:
From: Sophia Chen <sophia@foundryspark.io>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sponsorship ask — credits + milestone top-up for cohort pilot

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team at FoundrySpark is excited about Redwood. We’re launching a closed cohort pilot next month (10 startups, ~6 weeks each) and need a predictable credit package to get everyone through initial integration + a growth milestone top-up that triggers once three cohorts show active usage.

Ask: can Redwood provision an initial promo credit pool for the cohort (we estimate ~120k tokens equivalent across teams for the first 6 weeks) and commit to a milestone-based top-up (additional 200k tokens) if activation thresholds are met? We’re operating on limited runway and this would make a material difference.

Attached is a light usage forecast and the cohort onboarding plan (foundryspark_usage_plan.xlsx). If helpful, Contracts can follow up with standard contracting questions (contracts@foundryspark.io).

High level benefits for Redwood: cohort visibility for case studies, product feedback loop, and a marketplace listing after pilot if adoption is good.

Appreciate any flexibility — happy to hop on a quick call this week to align.

Best,
Sophia Chen
Founder & CEO | FoundrySpark
phone: +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Sophia Chen <sophia@foundryspark.io>
Cc: Contracts <contracts@foundryspark.io>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 11:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sponsorship ask — credits + milestone top-up for cohort pilot

Hi Sophia — great to meet you on the call.

Short answer: yes, we can do a provisional promo package and a milestone-based top-up, subject to a short approval flow and a mutually agreed activation definition. To move quickly I propose the following Mutual Action Plan (MAP):

- Initial: Redwood provisions a cohort promo bucket of 100k tokens (provisional).
- Activation metric: 3 startups in the cohort with >= 5k tokens/month AND one production inference integration (i.e., integration that hits Redwood API with auth).
- Milestone top-up: upon verification of activation, Redwood releases 200k additional tokens to the cohort bucket.
- Timing: verification window is 8 weeks from pilot start.
- Legal: sponsor SLA (two-page) to be signed; I’ll share a draft.

Questions from our side to confirm cost exposure:
1) Can you confirm the expected token mix (generative vs embeddings vs reranking)?
2) Any startups planning continuous streaming workloads (long conversations / high seq len)?
3) Will FoundrySpark require a marketplace promo code or direct account credits?

I’m looping in Contracts (cc) and will start an internal ping to get provisional approval. If you’re good with the MAP above I’ll send the sponsor SLA and draft HubSpot deal.

—Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Head of Growth | Redwood Inference
mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai

>
> Ask: can Redwood provision an initial promo credit pool for the cohort (we estimate ~120k tokens equivalent across teams for the first 6 weeks) and commit to a milestone-based top-up (additional 200k tokens) if activation thresholds are met?
>
> Attached is a light usage forecast and the cohort onboarding plan (foundryspark_usage_plan.xlsx).
>
> Best,
> Sophia


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: FoundrySpark sponsorship — provisional credits & milestone top-up — approval request

Marissa / Karthik,

Forwarding a sponsorship request from FoundrySpark (attached usage forecast). Short summary and request:

- Request: initial cohort promo pool ~100k tokens + milestone-based top-up of 200k tokens if activation metrics met.
- Business impact: high-touch cohort with case study potential and marketplace listing after pilot. Likely a small logo but strong GTM signal for developer ecosystem.
- Expected time window: pilot starts ~Oct 15, verification within 8 weeks.
- Estimated cost exposure (list price): initial ~ $900 (promo bucket discounted), top-up ~ $1,800 (if triggered). We would absorb as promo spend / partner budget.
- Deal ID: DL-4237-FS (will create HubSpot record once approved).

Ask: Approve a provisional promo allocation (100k tokens) and authority to commit to the milestone-topup framework above. If approved I’ll send Sponsor_SLA_v1 and route to Legal for quick sign.

Forwarded message (excerpt):
> From: Sophia Chen <sophia@foundryspark.io>
> Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
> Subject: Sponsorship ask — credits + milestone top-up for cohort pilot
>
> Can Redwood provision an initial promo credit pool for the cohort (we estimate ~120k tokens equivalent across teams for the first 6 weeks) and commit to a milestone-based top-up (additional 200k tokens) if activation thresholds are met?

Thanks — I’ll stand by for a quick yes/no so I can finalize the HubSpot deal and get Contracts in the loop.

—Mateo


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 14:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: FoundrySpark sponsorship — provisional credits & milestone top-up — approval request

Thanks for the summary, Mateo. I’ve reviewed the numbers and approve the provisional allocation on the following conditions:

1) Provisional promo bucket: 100k tokens, assigned to HubSpot deal DL-4237-FS, promo code label: cohort-2026-Q4.
2) Milestone top-up: 200k tokens conditional on verification (3 startups >= 5k tokens/month + one integration) within the 8-week window. We’ll cap the total promo exposure at 300k tokens for accounting.
3) Legal must sign Sponsor_SLA_v1 (attached) and Contracts must confirm no special IP or data residency terms.
4) Growth to own success comms and case study approvals.

Karthik — please flag if this is a problem for Reserved / Dedicated routing or if we need to add capacity SLA exceptions.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Head of Partnerships | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.ai

> Forwarding a sponsorship request from FoundrySpark (attached usage forecast). Short summary and request:...


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Sophia Chen <sophia@foundryspark.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Contracts <contracts@foundryspark.io>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sponsorship ask — credits + milestone top-up for cohort pilot (approved)

Hi Sophia — good news: we’ve provisionally approved the cohort sponsorship and milestone top-up framework. Summary and next steps below.

Summary of commitment:
- Initial credits: 100,000 tokens provisioned to the FoundrySpark cohort promo bucket (promo label: cohort-2026-Q4).
- Milestone top-up: 200,000 tokens reserved and released upon verification of the activation metric (3 startups >= 5k tokens/month + at least one integration calling Redwood API). Verification window: 8 weeks from pilot start.
- Total promo cap: 300,000 tokens.

Conditions:
- Sponsor_SLA_v1 must be signed (link / doc below). No custom IP/data residency clauses were approved — if FoundrySpark requires those we’ll need an enterprise review.
- Promo is for test/dev and early production only; standard TOS applies.

Action items:
1) I’ve uploaded Sponsor_SLA_v1 for signature: https://www.docusign.com/Sign?d=Sponsor_SLA_v1 (Contracts can countersign).
2) Please confirm pilot start date and token mix (gen / embeddings / rerank). We used the attached forecast to estimate exposure.
3) Once Contracts signs the SLA I’ll push the credits and create HubSpot deal DL-4237-FS with the promo bucket attached.

Attachments: Sponsor_SLA_v1.pdf (sponsor terms), usage_forecast.xlsx (internal copy).

We’re excited to work with FoundrySpark — happy to join the kickoff call and help with onboarding and best practices for cost controls (batching, prefix caching).

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Head of Growth | Redwood Inference
mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai
phone: +1 (650) 555-0133

title: Renewal: SOC 2 re-attestation — evidence routing, retention uplift and renewal timeline
preview:
From: Samantha Cho <s.cho@finovabank.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: SOC 2 re-attestation evidence needs and retention ask

Hi Mateo,

Our renewal with Redwood is coming up (contract anniversary: 2026-11-15) and Compliance is starting the SOC 2 re-attestation prep. Two immediate asks from our internal auditors:

1) Confirm the retention horizon you can support for audit logs (our target is 24 months) and any price impact for retention > 12 months.
2) A simple delivery option for bulk evidence (on-prem vs secure SFTP vs shared Drive) — we have an on-prem evidence intake process and need to know what format to expect (hashed logs, CSV index, manifest, etc.).

Can you provide a proposed evidence intake plan and a timeline for re-attestation artifacts? Ideally we need a mutual action plan and a doc we can hand to our audit team by 2026-09-24.

Also looping in procurement if there are contract amendments for retention or capacity uplift.

Thanks,
Samantha Cho
Senior Compliance Officer
Finova Bank

Attachments: none


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cho <s.cho@finovabank.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-16T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SOC 2 re-attestation evidence needs and retention ask

Hi Samantha — thanks for the heads up and the deadlines. Short answers up front: we can support 24 months of audit-log retention and we have two common delivery patterns for evidence handoff. Below is a proposed intake plan and timelines; I also attached an index template and retention matrix.

Quick TL;DR
- Retention: 24 months supported; retention uplift to >12 months typically incurs a modest monthly storage add-on (I included a ballpark in the matrix).
- Delivery options: (A) Secure SFTP (preferred for bulk raw logs), (B) Encrypted Drive binder with manifest + sha256 hashes (works for on-prem ingestion), (C) Direct API export for structured traces (if you want filtered slices).

Mutual action plan (proposed)
- 2026-09-17: Redwood provides evidence-index template (attached) + retention matrix.
- 2026-09-20: Finova confirms delivery path (SFTP vs Drive) and retention choice (12 vs 24 mo).
- 2026-09-27: Redwood begins secure transfer of first evidence tranche.
- 2026-10-15: Remaining evidence delivered; Redwood provides signed attestation bundle for auditor.

Evidence format / manifest (examples)
- CSV index with record types, time windows, file names, sha256 hash, and a short note about redaction. (see soc2-evidence-index.csv)
- Raw log files: gzip per day, per service, with metadata header.
- Audit trace extracts: JSONL or CSV depending on your auditor preference.

Cost/terms notes
- 24-month retention: estimated storage uplift $0.003/token-month (example calc in retention-matrix-v2.xlsx).
- If you want retention captured as a contract amendment we can route a term addendum; procurement will need the DocuSign link (I can send a draft).

Next steps
- Samantha: confirm desired retention and preferred transfer path by 2026-09-20.
- Kimberly/Vanessa: if there are operational constraints on our side (SFTP creds, retention provisioning), can you confirm feasibility by end of day 2026-09-18 so we can meet Finova's timeline?

Thanks — happy to jump on a short call if helpful.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
(attachment: soc2-evidence-index.csv)


From: Samantha Cho <s.cho@finovabank.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-22T15:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SOC 2 re-attestation evidence needs and retention ask

Hi Mateo — apologies for the slow reply, we were running the intake with our auditors this week. We agree to 24 months retention. A couple clarifications:

- Delivery: We prefer encrypted Drive binder with manifest and sha256 for anything that must stay in Finova custody; for large raw streams (application logs) we can accept SFTP.
- Data required: the auditors asked that request/response traces be pseudonymized (we can accept hashed user ids). Can you confirm Redwood can deliver hashed identifiers rather than raw PHI?
- Capacity question: If we increase retention to 24 months, will you recommend Dedicated GPU pool for any performance-sensitive export workloads, or is Hosted acceptable with a planned export window? If Dedicated is recommended, please include the uplift pricing and a DocuSign amendment.

Looping in procurement contact Rajesh Patel (cc) for contract amendment logistics.

On the timeline: we still need the mutual action plan in a format our auditors can consume by 2026-09-24. The Drive binder link and the index template will do.

Thanks,
Samantha

-----Original Message-----
On Sep 16, 2026, at 11:03 AM, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Samantha — thanks for the heads up and the deadlines. Short answers up front: we can support 24 months...



From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Finova renewal — internal runbook and next steps (evidence routing + DocuSign)

Team,

Recap and action items after Finova confirmed 24‑month retention and Drive/SFTP mix. This is internal only — I copied Jonas for pricing sign-off.

Recap
- Customer: Finova Bank (deal DEAL-8421), renewal anniversary 2026-11-15.
- Retention: 24 months (customer committed).
- Delivery: Encrypted Drive binder + manifest for custody-sensitive artifacts; SFTP for bulk raw logs. They want hashed IDs for request/response traces.

Operational tasks (who / when)
- Mateo: prepare evidence-upload-playbook.pdf with file naming conventions and sample manifest; share Drive binder template by 2026-10-04.
- Vanessa: provision SFTP endpoint and create one-time creds scoped to Finova; target 2026-10-06.
- Jonas: confirm pricing uplift for 24-month retention and confirm whether we should recommend Dedicated pool for export windows; if Dedicated recommended, please prepare DocuSign amendment draft (link in related_links) by 2026-10-06.
- Kimberly: coordinate final mutual action plan document and provide to Samantha by 2026-10-07 so auditors have time before renewal.

Attachments to upload to internal drive: soc2-evidence-index.csv, retention-matrix-v2.xlsx, evidence-upload-playbook.pdf

Notes: Samantha expects hashed identifiers — engineering can provide a hashing option in the export pipeline; Vanessa, please flag if that requires more than minor config.

Thanks — I’ll assemble the MAP once Jonas confirms pricing.

Kimberly Park
Head of Customer Security Operations
Redwood Inference

> > Hi Mateo — apologies for the slow reply, we were running the intake with our auditors this week. We agree to 24 months retention...

- Staged activation: propose a 3-step plan — (1) EU staging acceptance (48–72h smoke test), (2) soft traffic shift (10% for 7 days), (3) full cutover. Acceptance gating: successful inference latency & throughput tests + no data-plane errors.

- If there are discounts/terms for adding projects under the current enterprise agreement (we're near renewal)

Date: Mon, 21 Sep 2026 10:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick: adding 5 projects + ask on committed throughput and term

Hi Elena — thanks for the matrix, looks clear. Short answers up front: separate API keys per project is the right call for auditability and scoping; we can provision those in bulk.

Operational notes and a proposed plan:
- Keys: We can create named keys in a single request and seed them with role scopes (read-only embeddings, generation). Suggested format: luma-prod-{projectcode}-{env}.
- Lead time: bulk key creation and tagging -> 1 business day once we have the project codes + owner emails; automated script can handle 50 keys/minute.
- Throughput/commit: For predictable unit economics, recommend a 12mo term addendum to the current agreement that increases committed token throughput by 40% and includes an EU-region capacity reservation for peak windows. I can share a pricing sketch.
- Staged rollout: create keys for all five projects, enable two in canary for 2 weeks (low-latency SLO), then open to remaining three. This minimizes regression risk.

I'll loop in Marissa on pricing and Priya for contract wording. Ben will outline any quota or architecture gotchas.

Would you and procurement be available for a 30-min sync this week to align on terms and the timeline?

Best,
Mateo
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>

Date: Tue, 22 Sep 2026 08:33:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick: adding 5 projects + ask on committed throughput and term

Elena — quick tech notes from SE side:
- Namespacing keys per project is good. Also recommend enabling per-key rate-limits and per-key cost tags so your internal billing team can attribute cost by team.
- EU region: we'll pin the projects to eu-west-1 for latency. One operational caveat: cross-region KV cache transfer isn't automatic; if you plan for failover to us-west, expect a cold-start penalty (we can mitigate with pre-warming).
- Sizing: based on your matrix, to keep 95th percentile token latency under 250ms for chat we recommend reserving an additional 0.6x of your current committed throughput (we're proposing the 40% Mateo mentioned).

I'll prepare a short runbook for the canary that includes monitoring hooks (latency, token burn, cost per request).

-ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineering, Redwood Inference

title: Post-demo performance checkpoints — p95/p99 latency + streaming constraints
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@novamed.ai>, Tom Berger <tom.berger@novamed.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: performance checkpoints and requested evidence

Thanks for your time today — appreciate the deep dive. Quick recap and concrete asks so we can scope a short POC and answer the p95/p99 questions you raised.

High-level acceptance targets you called out on the call:
- Non-streaming text generation (sync API): p95 target ~200ms end-to-end, p99 acceptable up to ~500ms during steady traffic.
- Streaming scenarios (token-by-token): p95 target ~220ms for first token, streaming tail p99 target ~650ms (we discussed tail mitigation options).
- Throughput/concurrency: baseline 1k req/s sustained across tenants, bursts to 4–5k req/s; streaming concurrency up to ~300 concurrent sessions with average token-rate ~5 tokens/sec.

What we (Redwood) will provide this week:
- A short latency evidence packet (see attached redwood_p95_p99_matrix.pdf) summarizing host-region measurements and per-hop breakdown (edge, runtime, model).
- A proposed measurement plan and traffic shape for the POC (will share CSV with exact distributions).

Customer asks we noted from your side:
1) Include network RTT in the e2e measurement (client -> Redwood edge -> model -> client).
2) Validate both cold-start and warmed-cache behavior (we should capture KV-cache warmup and sustained steady-state).
3) Include mixed workload: ~60% streaming sessions, 40% sync requests; 10–15% long-context (16k tokens) tail fraction.

Next steps / asks from you (Novamed):
- Confirm the traffic profile above or share an anonymized 24h sample (CSV) of request counts, token lengths, and streaming fraction.
- Confirm legal / access requirements for a 1-week POC environment (we can run hosted evaluation under a short-term contract).
- Preferred kickoff slots next week (we can propose a window).

Attaching: redwood_p95_p99_matrix.pdf (summary).

Thanks — I’ll coordinate an internal SE run-through once we get your sample traffic.

— Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com

Attachments: redwood_p95_p99_matrix.pdf (summary table, region breakdown)

From: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@novamed.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Berger <tom.berger@novamed.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 18:13:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: performance checkpoints and requested evidence

Thanks for the succinct recap, Mateo — that matches our notes. A couple clarifications from our side before we lock the POC shape:

1) Our current provider shows: sync p95 ~185ms, p99 ~620ms (these numbers include app-side processing but exclude the last-mile mobile RTT). We need Redwood’s measurement to include true client-facing RTT so we can compare apples-to-apples.
2) For streaming, our users are sensitive to first-byte latency. Can you capture both first-byte and token-step latencies separately?
3) We want the POC to run behind our VPN (we’ll provide a short allowlist window) and to push both warm and cold cache traffic.

We can provide a 24h anonymized sample of traffic by EOD Monday. Also, we are fine with a 5-business-day POC; please propose the earliest window.

Olivia
Olivia Grant
Head of Product <Novamed AI>
olivia.grant@novamed.ai

---
Attachment: prospect_note.txt (short notes)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@novamed.ai>, Tom Berger <tom.berger@novamed.ai>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Sep 2026 08:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: performance checkpoints and requested evidence

Hi Olivia — Karthik from the Redwood infra team. Brief notes on how we’d operationalize the measurement so the POC is meaningful: 

Measurement approach (e2e):
- We measure from an instrumented client to the client-facing edge (TLS handshake RTT included) through our routing layer, runtime, model execution, and back to the client. We’ll timestamp at ingress and final byte-out for each request/stream chunk.
- Streaming metrics: we record first-byte latency (time to first chunk) and per-chunk inter-arrival distribution to measure token pacing and tail.

Key config knobs that affect p99 tails (and what we’ll control in the POC):
- Batch size & max-latency thresholds in the inference runtime (we’ll provide a deterministic tuning guide).
- Prefetch/KV cache warmup policy: for a cold-cache phase we’ll simulate realistic cold-hit fraction and then run a warmed steady-state window.
- Priority routing: we can isolate a dedicated pool for Novamed traffic during the POC to remove noisy co-tenants.

Proposed acceptance criteria for the POC (draft):
- Sync text-gen: p95 <= 220ms (client-visible), p99 <= 550ms in steady-state.
- Streaming: first-byte p95 <= 240ms; streaming tail p99 <= 700ms for >=95% of sessions (we’ll document the tail distribution).
- Throughput: sustain 1k req/s with burst handling to 4k for 5m windows without p95 regressions >20%.
- Availability: 99.9% of requests complete with no 5xx error for the POC window.

Attached is a measurement-plan.csv stub that outlines exact traffic distributions (streaming fraction, long-context tail, token-length bins). If Novamed provides the anonymized 24h sample we can canonicalize these bins and produce a daily traffic script.

If you confirm the acceptance criteria above, we’ll schedule the infra prep and provide a short runbook for your VPN allowlist. Proposed POC window: Tue 2026-09-22 through Mon 2026-09-28.

Thanks,
Karthik
Staff Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik_iyer@redwood.com

Attachments: measurement-plan.csv

From: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@novamed.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Berger <tom.berger@novamed.ai>
Date: Sat, 19 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: performance checkpoints and requested evidence

Appreciate the detail, Karthik. The acceptance criteria look reasonable to us — please proceed. We’ll send the anonymized 24h sample by EOD Monday.

Let’s kick off the POC on Tue 2026-09-22 at 11:00 AM PT — I’ve reserved a slot: https://calendly.com/olivia-grant/novamed-redwood-poc-kickoff.

Olivia

---

- Streaming metrics: we record first-byte latency (time to first chunk) and per-chunk inter-arrival distribution to measure token pacing and tail.

Key config knobs that affect p99 tails (and what we’ll control in the POC):
- Batch size & max-latency thresholds in the inference runtime (we’ll provide a deterministic tuning guide).
- Prefetch/KV cache warmup policy: for a cold-cache phase we’ll simulate realistic cold-hit fraction and then run a warmed steady-state window.
- Priority routing: we can isolate a dedicated pool for Novamed traffic during the POC to remove noisy co-tenants.

Proposed acceptance criteria for the POC (draft):
- Sync text-gen: p95 <= 220ms (client-visible), p99 <= 550ms in steady-state.
- Streaming: first-byte p95 <= 240ms; streaming tail p99 <= 700ms for >=95% of sessions (we’ll document the tail distribution).
- Throughput: sustain 1k req/s with burst handling to 4k for 5m windows without p95 regressions >20%.
- Availability: 99.9% of requests complete with no 5xx error for the POC window.

Attached is a measurement-plan.csv stub that outlines exact traffic distributions (streaming fraction, long-context tail, token-length bins). If Novamed provides the anonymized 24h sample we can canonicalize these bins and produce a daily traffic script.

If you confirm the acceptance criteria above, we’ll schedule the infra prep and provide a short runbook for your VPN allowlist. Proposed POC window: Tue 2026-09-22 through Mon 2026-09-28.

Thanks,
Karthik
Staff Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik_iyer@redwood.com

Attachments: measurement-plan.csv

Subject: Discovery — HealthSync inference priorities

Hi Mateo —

Thanks for taking time today. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so we can scope a clean pilot:

- Primary workload: summarization + structured extraction from multi-section clinical notes (avg 850 tokens, peaks ~3k).
- Must-haves: p99 latency <= 450ms for interactive reviewer flows; sustained TPS for batch ingest ~18 TPS during nightly windows.
- Quality constraints: extremely low hallucination tolerance on medication and allergies fields.
- Compliance: SOC 2 evidence and data retention options required by procurement.

Can you share recommended models + perf expectations (TPS, p50/p90/p99 latency) and any sample prompt sets you’d want to test? Procurement also asked for a tentative cost estimate per 1M tokens.

Best,
Asha
Asha Mehta
Head of Product, HealthSync AI


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Asha Mehta <asha@healthsync.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-15T09:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Discovery — HealthSync inference priorities

Thanks Asha — great notes. Quick post-call recap and an initial recommendation set so your team can review before we lock a pilot scope. (Forwarding below for context.)

Summary / immediate recs:
- Recommended starting slate: Redwood-mistral-8k-q4 (quantized), Redwood-llama2-13b-int8, and an experiment with Redwood-flare-7b (low-latency).
  - Why these: balance between factuality for extraction (13b) and latency/cost for reviewer flows (7b/8k quantized).
- Verified perf snapshot (attached CSV & Drive link): per-model sustained TPS and latency percentiles measured on n1-standard equivalent with continuous batching configured to your sequence profile. Key lines pulled out below:
  - Redwood-mistral-8k-q4 — sustained TPS: 22 TPS; p50: 85ms; p90: 210ms; p99: 390ms
  - Redwood-llama2-13b-int8 — sustained TPS: 12 TPS; p50: 120ms; p90: 300ms; p99: 520ms
  - Redwood-flare-7b — sustained TPS: 30 TPS; p50: 60ms; p90: 150ms; p99: 270ms

Notes on quality/regression risk:
- Extraction fidelity is model-dependent. We’ve seen occasional entity linking regressions when prompts over-index on conversational framing vs structured extraction. We recommend a regression guardrail set (sample prompts + failure cases) before any rollout.

Attachments provided: redwood-verified-perfpack-20260917.csv (full traces), hs-promptset-v1.txt (starter prompt templates), healthsync-cost-estimate-draft.pdf (ballpark tokens-to-cost). Also uploaded the perfpack to Drive: https://drive.redwood.ai/HealthSync/PerfPack/verified-perfpack-20260917

Suggested next steps:
1) Technical deep-dive (30–45m) to align on sequence distribution and sampling prompts — propose Tue 2026-09-21 10:00 or Wed 2026-09-22 15:00 PT.
2) Ben will prepare a short targeted run with your top-20 clinical notes (anonymized) to capture hallucination surface and end-to-end latency.
3) Procurement: we’ll share SOC 2 evidence and a simple procurement checklist for Dedicated vs Hosted options.

If that looks good I’ll lock the TD with Ben and Jonas on our side.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Enterprise Account Exec | Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>

Subject: Integrator awayday workshop — hands-on technical lab & certification proposal

Hi Elaine,

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our discussion, attached is a 2-day hands-on technical bootcamp proposal designed to certify Northstar consultants on Redwood Inference fundamentals and deployment patterns (prefix/KV caching, batching, model variant routing, and private deployment onboarding). High-level objectives: 

- Get 10–15 consultants through a repeatable, vendor-verified lab that results in a \"Redwood Certified Integrator (Foundations)\" badge.
- Validate each team can deploy a sample end-to-end pipeline (Hosted -> Private fallback) and run perf tests.
- Provide a joint mutual action plan for ramp and follow-on enablement (Office hours, shadow delivery).

Proposed schedule (day-by-day, high level):
Day 1 — Core concepts + hands-on labs (runtime, routing policies, KV cache)
Day 2 — Troubleshooting, ops, private deployment sandbox + certification exercise

I've attached the proposal (redwood-bootcamp-proposal-v1.pdf) and an initial lab environment checklist (lab-environment-reqs.xlsx). Can you confirm:
1) Target attendee headcount (10–15 recommended).
2) Preferred dates in late Sep / early Oct (we suggested Sep 28–29 as an example).
3) Any PO/contracting constraints or minimum invoicing requirements we should align to.

If that works, I'll loop in our delivery leads (Laura / Soojin) to lock trainers and environment provisioning.

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Partner Programs | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.ai

Immediate plan (short-term workaround):
1) I’ll generate a temporary Zoom meeting for the demo and paste the join link into the event description and top of the invite. This avoids the accept flow entirely for guests.
2) Camila will re-send a cleaned-up invite authored from my mailbox (mateo@redwood.com) so organizer shows as a Redwood individual instead of the service account.
3) Ben — can your team confirm whether the Redwood Calendar integration will reissue a fresh ICS without stripping attendees? If not, we’ll send an explicit calendar attachment (ICS) and the Zoom link.

I’m working on the Zoom link now and will attach the Redwood one-pager and a meeting ICS. If we need to nudge HarborTech IT to allow contractor accounts, let me know and we’ll provide the event headers for their admin to whitelist.

Proposed fallback event: 2026-09-16 10:00 PT (same slot).

Thanks — Mateo

If you can send the first tranche by EOD 2026-09-11 well begin our internal review and lock a 2026-09-20 target for final sign-off.

Claire

title: Re: Proposal for converting burst capacity to reserved pool + pricing options
preview:
From: Sera Lin <sera@reticle.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Becker <tom.becker@reticle.ai>
Date: Tue, 7 Sep 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Converting burst credits into reserved capacity — need options

Hi Mateo,

Quick context: we ran a larger-than-expected pilot in August and burned ~3x our burst credits. Cost surprised our finance team and they asked if we can convert a portion of that spend into a reserved pool to lower per-token cost going forward.

As we head into renewal in Nov, we want to explore:
- A mid-contract conversion of X tokens of historical burst into a reserved commitment for the remaining 14 months
- A blended discount structure that recognizes the usage history (so we don't double-bill)
- Any changes to SLA or priority that come with reserved capacity

Can you share the options and a rough cost comparison? If possible, can we get a one-page that shows: current spend vs proposed commit, net monthly change, and a recommended path (annualize vs staged conversion)?

Thanks — Sera
Sera Lin
Head of Platform, Reticle.ai

P.S. attaching the last invoice and our usage export for August.

--- Attachment: reticle_august_usage.csv (csv)
--- Attachment: invoice_20260831.pdf (pdf)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Sera Lin <sera@reticle.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Sep 2026 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Converting burst credits into reserved capacity — need options

Hi Sera — thanks for the detail and the files. I took a first pass and attached a model with three pragmatic options:

1) Transition-Reserve (recommended): Convert 60% of the August burst spend into a 14-month reserved pool at a 28% blended discount vs current pay-as-you-go. Preserves current routing and priority.
2) Staged-Annualize: Convert 100% into a 6-month staged reserve that ramps from 40% to 100% commitment — slightly lower effective discount (~22%) but more flexibility.
3) Credit+Topup (quick fix): Issue a one-off credit to smooth August invoice and maintain current burst-only pattern; no change to pricing going forward.

I've included a simple cashflow table and a delta vs current monthly burn. The proposed Transition-Reserve gives you the largest per-token savings and smooths finance planning; the staged approach is easier for procurement/legal but costs a bit more.

Jordan and Kim — can you confirm if we can offer a 28% blended discount on the Transition-Reserve and whether legal needs any amendments to the SOW for a mid-contract commit? Also, if we apply a credit for the quick-fix approach, what guardrails do we need so it doesn't set a precedent?

Sera — attaching the model (reticle_pricing_model_v2.xlsx) and a one-page summary (reticle_proposal_v1.pdf). Happy to hop on a call tomorrow.

Best,
Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
--- Attachment: reticle_pricing_model_v2.xlsx (xlsx)
--- Attachment: reticle_proposal_v1.pdf (pdf)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 8 Sep 2026 10:32:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Converting burst credits into reserved capacity — need options

Mateo / Kim — looping in procurement/legal pathing notes. High level thoughts:

- Pricing: 28% blended on a 14-month commit feels tight but defensible given their spike and clear runway. Approve conditionally if we can tie it to a minimum monthly drawdown (to avoid idling capacity). Suggest Minimum Monthly Drawdown = 40% of committed tokens for first 3 months, then relax to 25%.
- SLA/Priority: No SLA uplift for this mid-contract change — keep same priority tier. If Reticle wants higher priority, that should be a separate SKU.
- Contract: Legal will need an amendment (Amendment A) that captures: start date of converted commit, invoice phasing, and the drawdown guardrail. I can sign off on commercial if Legal signs the amendment.
- Credit policy: Avoid issuing a general-purpose credit. If we do one-off, mark it as one-time invoice adjustment and require Exec approval.

Kim — can you draft the amendment template (short form) and confirm currency/PO flow? Mateo — if you can get Sera's internal PO cadence, we can line up invoicing.

-Jordan
VP, Revenue Operations

Thanks Jordan. I drafted a short amendment and a billing phasing schedule. Key points:

- Amendment A will be 2 pages: definition of converted tokens, effective start date (date of signature), invoicing schedule (monthly amortization), and drawdown requirement (as suggested).
- If Reticle signs within 30 days, we can backdate the start-of-conversion to Oct 1 and apply the phased invoicing described in the model.
- PO flow: Reticle uses Net 30. We will issue monthly invoices against the amortized amount.
- Legal flags: need to confirm that the conversion doesn't change data residency or access controls; added a single clause to state "no change to service boundaries or data handling".

I've attached the draft amendment and the billing appendix. Jordan — please confirm sign-off and I will hand to Legal for a quick review.

Cheers,
Kimberly Park

title: API Compatibility Window: impact triage & comms slot request
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-09-02T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: API Compatibility Window: impact triage & comms slot request

Hi team,

We need a quick cross-functional triage for the planned deprecation of the v1/embed endpoint we announced in the product roadmap (target retire: 2027-01-15). Acme is our largest embed customer in the pilot cohort and they've flagged potential downstream risk if we keep the default quantized variant only.

Ask: can Eng/Product confirm a 6-week compatibility window (traffic routed to variant v1.1 with a compatibility shim) + a single customer comms slot during the week of 2026-10-05? Specifically I need:
- Product: confirm the acceptance criteria for the shim and any known behavior differences to include in the customer note.
- Eng: high-level ETA and constraints for the shim rollout and whether it requires infra capacity reservations.
- CS/Sales (Marissa): are there contractual constraints for Acme that would force a longer runway?

I've attached a preliminary impact matrix (migration_impact_matrix.xlsx) with customer callouts. Please reply with your availability for a 30m triage tomorrow or Wednesday.

Thanks,
Mateo
Sr. Program Manager, Product Ops
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Lara Chen <lara.chen@acme.com>
Date: 2026-09-02T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: API Compatibility Window: impact triage & comms slot request

Thanks Mateo — I can take the lead on the customer coordination side. Quick notes from Acme:
- They asked for a formal exemption intake to be filled and signed by their procurement team (I attached the PDF they emailed us).
- From the commercial side, Acme is asking for a 90-day notice minimum for any behavioral change to embeddings (they referenced legacy SOW language).

I recommend we run the triage with Eng/Product, then schedule a 1:1 with Acme to confirm the impact matrix rows that matter to them. I'm free tomorrow 11:30–12:30 or Wednesday 09:00–10:00.

Marissa
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-09-02T12:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: API Compatibility Window: impact triage & comms slot request

Adding Product POV:
- Acceptance criteria for the shim: parity on top-k similarity distribution ±2% for 99th percentile queries, consistent embedding dimensionality, no change to request/response contract. Any semantic drift must be documented.
- If we can't meet parity, we should offer an opt-in stable variant (v1.1-stable) for customers with contracts. That would require a policy and rollout plan but is feasible.

I'm available tomorrow 11:30. Marcus — can SRE confirm capacity implications if we maintain the shim for a customer lane?

Hannah
Director, Product


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-09-02T15:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: API Compatibility Window: impact triage & comms slot request

On Wed, Sep 2, 2026 at 12:47 PM Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com> wrote:
> Adding Product POV:
> - Acceptance criteria for the shim: parity on top-k similarity distribution ±2% for 99th percentile queries, consistent embedding dimensionality, no change to request/response contract. Any semantic drift must be documented.
>
> I'm available tomorrow 11:30. Marcus — can SRE confirm capacity implications if we maintain the shim for a customer lane?

Quick SRE check-in: a customer-specific shim running concurrently with the default path will need a pinned worker pool initially (2-4 GPUs depending on QPS). We can autoscale after we verify traffic shape but there is a 3-week lead time to guarantee dedicated pool availability in us-west-2 (procurement + infra provisioning). Ops cost estimate: ~$12k/month while pinned.

I'll join tomorrow 11:30 and bring a short cost/ETA slide.

Marcus Lin
Principal SRE, Serving Runtime


From: Lara Chen <lara.chen@acme.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: platform@acme.com
Date: 2026-09-03T09:06:00-07:00
Subject: Re: API Compatibility Window: impact triage & comms slot request

Hi all,

Thanks for the quick turnaround. A couple of clarifications from Acme's side:
- Procurement will only sign an exemption if the compatibility window is at least 60 days and we have a rollback plan documented.
- We need an internal test harness to validate semantic parity for our top 5 production prompts; can Redwood provide sample outputs or a reproducible runbook?
- If dedicated capacity is required, we will need a cost offset conversation (we have volume commitments that justify it).

We can join tomorrow during your triage — please confirm final time.

Best,
Lara Chen
Head of Platform, Acme, Inc.


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Lara Chen <lara.chen@acme.com>
Date: 2026-09-09T16:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: API Compatibility Window: impact triage & comms slot request

Team — summary & next steps after today's triage:

1) Decision: we will open a 6-week compatibility window for Acme (2026-10-01 -> 2026-11-11) under a signed exemption. Product will publish a short behavior note (Hannah).
2) Eng/SRE (Marcus): provision a pinned pool in us-west-2; target readiness 2026-09-23 for internal smoke tests. Cost estimate shared in the triage: ~$12k/month; Finance to flag any compensatory credits request.
3) CS/Sales (Marissa): draft exemption and coordinate procurement signatures with Acme (90-day commercial ask is waived for this specific pilot pending signed intake).
4) Delivery: provide Acme a test harness + sample outputs (Action: Marcus + Eng by 2026-09-20).
5) Comms slot: we will send the customer-facing note the week of 2026-10-05; internal comms to infra & support on 2026-09-27.

I've uploaded the final impact matrix and the signed intake template to the shared doc: https://docs.redwood.com/compatibility/intake-form. Attaching the final XLS and the Acme PDF they provided.

If anyone objects or needs a different runway by EOD 2026-09-11, flag it here.

Thanks,
Mateo
Sr. Program Manager, Product Ops
Redwood Inference

title: Renewal negotiation: NebulaWorks — tiered surge credits & commit cadence
preview:
From: Claire Hsu <claire.hsu@nebulaworks.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Tom Ruiz <tom.ruiz@nebulaworks.com>
Cc: procurement@nebulaworks.com
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal planning — looking to balance commit and burst credits

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the trial support during Q2 — usage spiked as expected and the team is happy with latency. Our renewal window is approaching (contract ends 2026-12-31) and procurement asked me to start the pricing conversation early. Key asks from NebulaWorks:

- Keep the standard per-token rate for committed usage, but give us a mechanism to handle unpredictable spikes (burst credits or short-term uplift).
- A price-hold for the first 12 months of the renewed term (we need budget certainty).
- A path to increase committed capacity mid-term if we hit growth faster than forecast.

I attached a small usage extract for Q2 to give a sense of spike frequency (NebulaWorks_usage_Q2-2026.csv).

Can you outline a couple of practical options (simple, exec-friendly) so I can present to procurement and engineering? Ideally one conservative option and one more aggressive upsell.

Appreciate it — Claire
NebulaWorks — Head of Platform Partnerships

P.S. procurement@nebulaworks.com should be looped for any legal/PO-related steps.

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Claire Hsu <claire.hsu@nebulaworks.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 26 Aug 2026 08:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal planning — looking to balance commit and burst credits

Claire — thanks for the clear asks and the Q2 extract. A couple of quick options that map to the account goals you described:

Option A — Conservative (budget-first, lower risk)
- 12-month price-hold on the committed per-token rate.
- 20% pre-paid credit applied to the first two invoice periods (applies against committed usage first).
- True-up quarterly with a 30-day invoice lag. If committed usage grows >25% QoQ, we trigger a mid-term commit review.

Option B — Growth-first (upsell-friendly)
- 12-month price-hold on current committed rate.
- 40% surge credit pool (one-time credit). Structure: 60% available as immediate credit to offset committed overage; 40% reserved as "surge buffer" that can be redeemed at a 1.5x consumed rate when used for short bursts.
- Commit cadence adjustment: initial 6-month committed baseline with an automatic review at month 6 to convert some of the reserved surge buffer into committed capacity at a pre-agreed conversion rate.
- If NebulaWorks signs a 12-month Dedicated reservation (GPU pool), we can increase the immediate credit by +10 percentage points and prioritize capacity in burst windows.

Notes / asks for you:
- We need an internal target committed ARR to size any Dedicated offer ($/month). Do you have a target monthly committed spend (ballpark)?
- Any regulatory or security paperwork required for Dedicated (VPC) vs Hosted? Evelyn should help on that.

I'll draft a concise amendment describing both options for procurement and include a concrete redline if you want to proceed.

Attachments referenced: NebulaWorks_usage_Q2-2026.csv (from your email)

- Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Enterprise Account Executive, Redwood Inference

On Tue, 25 Aug 2026 at 09:04 AM Claire Hsu <claire.hsu@nebulaworks.com> wrote:
> Hi Mateo,
>
> Thanks again for the trial support during Q2 — usage spiked as expected and the team is happy with latency. Our renewal window is approaching (contract ends 2026-12-31) and procurement asked me to start the pricing conversation early. Key asks from NebulaWorks:
>
> - Keep the standard per-token rate for committed usage, but give us a mechanism to handle unpredictable spikes (burst credits or short-term uplift).
> - A price-hold for the first 12 months of the renewed term (we need budget certainty).
> - A path to increase committed capacity mid-term if we hit growth faster than forecast.
>
> I attached a small usage extract for Q2 to give a sense of spike frequency (NebulaWorks_usage_Q2-2026.csv).
>
> Can you outline a couple of practical options (simple, exec-friendly) so I can present to procurement and engineering? Ideally one conservative option and one more aggressive upsell.
>
> Appreciate it — Claire
> NebulaWorks — Head of Platform Partnerships

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 27 Aug 2026 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal planning — looking to balance commit and burst credits

Thanks Mateo — both options read well for different exec audiences. Quick internal take from me and legal ops:

- We can approve Option B (Growth-first) for negotiation if NebulaWorks commits to a minimum $150k/month baseline (first 6 months). That baseline makes the 40% surge-credit economically feasible on our side.
- Condition the conversion of reserved surge credit to committed capacity on a documented month-6 usage review with an agreed conversion rate (we propose 0.9x effective conversion to protect against churn).
- Add a standard true-up and audit clause; procurement likes a max lookback of 12 months for usage verification.
- If they prefer not to lock $150k/mo, offer a split: 25% immediate credit + option to buy the remainder of the surge pool at a discounted rate within Q1 of the new term.

I'll attach a short amendment draft (proposed_amendment_v1.docx) that codifies: 12-month price-hold, 40% surge pool mechanics, month-6 conversion guardrails, and the $150k baseline.

Ask to Mateo: please confirm whether we should include a Dedicated-pricing appendix (if they show interest in Dedicated we'll need to route pricing through Markus for GPU sizing/priority).

- Monica
Director, Customer Success Operations, Redwood Inference

--- forward with edits ---

Date: Wed, 26 Aug 2026 08:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal planning — looking to balance commit and burst credits

(See original message above)

From: Claire Hsu <claire.hsu@nebulaworks.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Ruiz <tom.ruiz@nebulaworks.com>, procurement@nebulaworks.com
Date: Wed, 1 Sep 2026 16:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal planning — looking to balance commit and burst credits

Thanks Mateo / Monica — this is helpful and close to what procurement was hoping to see. A couple of follow-ups from our side before we escalate to legal for redlines:

1) Can you confirm SLA behavior for burst windows (if we opt into Dedicated priority) — expected RPS and any blackout windows?
2) For the $150k/month proposal, can you send a short illustrative invoice cadence and an example true-up math so procurement can model cashflow?
3) If we take the split alternative (25% immediate + buy the rest in Q1), what discount would you offer on purchasing the remainder?

Procurement asked that we get a single consolidated amendment showing the preferred option; we prefer Option B with the $150k baseline but want the split alternative as a fallback. Once we agree on language, procurement will route to DocuSign.

Action items for Redwood: consolidated redline + an example invoice/true-up table + confirmation on Dedicated SLA language (if Dedicated is included).

I'll wait for the redline and example math; happy to have a short call next week if helpful.

Thanks,
Claire
Head of Platform Partnerships, NebulaWorks

title: Re-anchor request: MAP adjustments + procurement bridge
preview:
From: Elena Park <elena.park@acmeinc.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Markus Lindstrom <markus.lindstrom@acmeinc.com>
Date: 2026-08-29T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Need to re-anchor MAP — evaluation delays on our side

Mateo,

Quick note from our end — the internal evaluation gate at Acme hit two blockers this week: the security team needs the live SOC2 evidence (they flagged a logging nuance) and our data infra team has delayed access to the test VPC until next week. Between the two we now expect a 10–14 day push on the original acceptance schedule unless we re-scope.

preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 31 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Monthly KPI dashboard — leadership readout readiness & owner action items

Team — attaching the Aug monthly KPI pack (PDF) and an editable owner tracker. This is the condensed set I plan to use in the leadership readout next Wednesday. High level notes:

- Latency: p95 inference latency improved 6% vs July (batching / KV cache changes). Still seeing a thin tail during APAC spikes (see slide 5).
- Cost: token cost per 1k decreased 4% after quantization rollout on v2 models, but embedding costs are up 12% driven by increased ingestion from a single large customer (Acme Cloud).
- Uptime: 99.96% for infra; one incident on Aug 12 with a fallback purple path — root cause tracked in ENG-1298.
- Usage: prod QPS up 24% month-over-month; 3 customers account for 58% of spikes in the evening window (PST).

Asks for prep: please review slides 4–7 and add owner names + short remediation text in the owner_action_tracker spreadsheet by EOD Tuesday. Specific owner asks below: 

- Latency tail in APAC (slide 5): need an owner for a short mitigation plan (route by region + priority queue policy). Suggested owner: Jonas.
- Embedding cost spike (slide 6): coordinate with Acme to understand ingestion delta and propose smoothing or batching controls. Suggested owner: Irene; we should ask Acme for their ingestion timeseries.
- Incident follow-through (slide 7): confirm that follow-up checklist is done (postmortem comments, alerts updated). Suggested owner: Soojin.

If you have any last-minute changes to the slides or want a different framing in the exec readout, flag them in the Drive link. I will circulate a speaker plan on Wednesday morning.

Attachments: monthly_kpi_dashboard_aug2026.pdf, owner_action_tracker_Aug2026.xlsx

Thanks —
Mateo
Sr. PM, Platform
Redwood Inference

Also, suggest we add a one-slide risk heatmap for the QBR to call out top two financial exposure areas (embeddings and burst traffic). That will make the ask clearer for leadership.

On the owner_action_tracker: I'm adding estimated ETA and a simple success metric for each remediation (e.g., reduce p95 tail by 20% during APAC peak).

Onward —

Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Anita Rao <procure@skyline.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 29 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Missing serials / spend caps on onboarding batch

Hi Mateo,

We're wrapping up our vendor onboarding for the August batch and ran into a blocking item: several provisional purchase serials you issued don't have the final PO numbers mapped and a handful of line items are missing spend-cap assignments. Our AP team can't finalize the payment terms without the canonical PO numbers and associated spend caps.

title: Clarifying controller/processor responsibilities & support access model
preview:
From: Asha Rao <asha@finco.io>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 13:15:00 -0400
Subject: Follow-up: demo — data handling & support access questions

Thanks again for the quick demo today — Mateo. A few items came up on our side that we'd like clarified before we move to procurement: 

1) Controller vs processor: we need to confirm which party is the data controller for customer-generated PII in the hosted offering, and whether Redwood will accept processor terms (we have a standard DPA).

2) Data residency: FinCo is US-only for PII. Do you have any controls to guarantee data and backups remain in-region for hosted?

3) Support/engineering access: we assume Redwood will have scoped access for debugging. Please describe break-glass procedures, how access is logged, and whether on-demand session recordings or just audit logs are available.

4) Retention & deletion: our default is retain only derived metadata for 30 days, raw inputs must be deletable on request. How fast can you delete a customer's data set and what evidence do you provide?

I've attached a high-level SOC2 matrix (placeholder) and included Daniel from security/ops and procurement on cc to surface any contract constraints. Can you send back a concise response we can share internally (security + procurement) — ideally a 1-pager and an example clause for processor responsibilities if you accept that model.

Best,
Asha
Asha Rao
Head of Security Architecture | FinCo Technologies
---
Attachment: SOC2-Matrix.xlsx (see stub)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Asha Rao <asha@finco.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 16:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: demo — data handling & support access questions

Asha — really appreciate the clear list. Quick recap and proposed next steps below so procurement/security have a single doc to react to.

Controller/Processor:
- Redwood is flexible: for hosted API customers we commonly operate as a processor when customers require it. We can sign your DPA with processor terms; we also offer a controller model for self-hosted/private deployments.
- Action: we'll draft a short DPA addendum showing processor language (data processing purposes, sub-processing, deletion obligations).

Data residency:
- Hosted: customers can pin region at account level (us-east-1 / us-west-2) and we persist primary and backup snapshots only in selected region(s) unless cross-region replication is explicitly enabled.
- Evidence: we can provide an export of storage bucket locations and a configuration snapshot.

Support access & break-glass:
- Normal support: role-based access inside a scoped support environment (JIT ephemeral keys) with session start/stop time and operator noted.
- Break-glass: requires customer approval via an ownership route (SAML SSO admin or a registered emergency contact); all break-glass sessions spawn an elevated audit event, are time-limited (default 2 hours), and produce an automated post-session report to the customer's security mailbox.
- Logs & recordings: we persist detailed audit logs (immutable append-only store) for 365 days by default; session recordings (video/terminal capture) are optional and can be enabled per-account.

Retention & deletion:
- We support object-level deletion plus account-level purge. Typical SLA: 72 hours to delete the primary index and remove model/feature caches; full storage wipe including backups completed within 30 days unless legal hold applies.
- Evidence: we provide a deletion certificate and storage manifest proving removed object hashes.

What I'll send you by EOD:
- 1-pager summarizing the points above tailored to FinCo (for S&R internal review)
- Draft DPA addendum language (processor-oriented)
- Example redwood-access-diagram.png showing support role boundaries (attached)

Next steps: could we schedule a 30-min call with your security and procurement (Daniel + procurement) tomorrow morning to run the DPA language and retention SLAs? Monica (cc) will own the follow-up and collate the evidence pack.

Thanks — Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
---
Attachments: redwood-access-diagram.png (stub)

From: Daniel Kim <daniel.k@finco.io>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Asha Rao <asha@finco.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Procurement Team <procure@finco.io>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: demo — data handling & support access questions

Thanks, Mateo. Couple clarifications from our side: 

- Processor model is preferable for hosted, so please confirm you can accept standard FinCo DPA clauses around subprocessing and breach notification timelines (we require 72 hours).
- For break-glass we need an auditable approval trail that doesn't rely on emailed approvals. Ideally an API-driven approval token or an SSO-based emergency flow. Is that supported?
- Retention: 30-day deletion window for backups is a hard requirement for PII — can you provide a customer-facing SLA clause we can insert into the master services schedule?

If you can provide the 1-pager + DPA excerpt before EOD we'll route to legal and procurement. If not, can we get a firm timeline for those artifacts and a 30-min slot tomorrow at 10:30am ET?

Regards,
Daniel Kim
Director, Security Operations | FinCo Technologies
---
Attachment: None

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Kim <daniel.k@finco.io>, Asha Rao <asha@finco.io>

Subject: Re: Follow-up: demo — data handling & support access questions

Asha — really appreciate the clear list. Quick recap and proposed next steps below so procurement/security have a single doc to react to.

Controller/Processor:
- Redwood is flexible: for hosted API customers we commonly operate as a processor when customers require it. We can sign your DPA with processor terms; we also offer a controller model for self-hosted/private deployments.
- Action: we'll draft a short DPA addendum showing processor language (data processing purposes, sub-processing, deletion obligations).

Data residency:
- Hosted: customers can pin region at account level (us-east-1 / us-west-2) and we persist primary and backup snapshots only in selected region(s) unless cross-region replication is explicitly enabled.
- Evidence: we can provide an export of storage bucket locations and a configuration snapshot.

Support access & break-glass:
- Normal support: role-based access inside a scoped support environment (JIT ephemeral keys) with session start/stop time and operator noted.
- Break-glass: requires customer approval via an ownership route (SAML SSO admin or a registered emergency contact); all break-glass sessions spawn an elevated audit event, are time-limited (default 2 hours), and produce an automated post-session report to the customer's security mailbox.
- Logs & recordings: we persist detailed audit logs (immutable append-only store) for 365 days by default; session recordings (video/terminal capture) are optional and can be enabled per-account.

Retention & deletion:
- We support object-level deletion plus account-level purge. Typical SLA: 72 hours to delete the primary index and remove model/feature caches; full storage wipe including backups completed within 30 days unless legal hold applies.
- Evidence: we provide a deletion certificate and storage manifest proving removed object hashes.

What I'll send you by EOD:
- 1-pager summarizing the points above tailored to FinCo (for S&R internal review)
- Draft DPA addendum language (processor-oriented)
- Example redwood-access-diagram.png showing support role boundaries (attached)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>

Date: Mon, 23 Aug 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Decision: opt-out window + termination for convenience — redline packet

Team — quick legal read and recommendation below (see redline_summary.xlsx for clause-by-clause):

Summary of options (trimmed):
- Option A (recommended): Accept 60-day opt-out notice on Order Form; restrict TFC payments to pre-paid unused term credits only (no early termination fee). Add a narrow carve-in for pilot credits to avoid unbilled consumption disputes.
- Option B (conservative): Push customer to 90-day opt-out + a modest TFC fee (1 month of committed spend) to cover reallocation and potential SLA disruptions.

Redline notes (excerpts):
- MSA §7 (Termination for Convenience): propose replacing blanket TFC with: \"Customer may terminate for convenience with written notice; Redwood's liability limited to refund of unearned pre-paid fees and reasonable costs to unwind Dedicated capacity (capped at one month of committed fees).\"
- Order Form §4 (Auto-renew): clarify that auto-renew triggers only apply to active paid subscriptions; trial/pilot periods must provide 30-day handback and no auto-renew.

Rationale: Option A balances customer flexibility and our reallocation costs; operational handback obligations should be in the Order Form and accompanied by a short SLA for data export/retention.

If you want a hybrid: accept 60-day opt-out but add a 30-day extended-handback fee if they request additional exported state or long-term retention beyond 30 days. I've attached a draft decision memo (optout_decision_memo.pdf) for exec sign-off.

Karthik
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference

From: Samir Patel <samir@lumenforge.com>

Subject: Kickoff: Redwood <> LumenForge connector — tech validation kickoff

Hi Mateo / Marissa / Jonas,

Thanks again for taking time today. As discussed on the call, LumenForge is aiming to validate the reference integration with Redwood in a staging environment before we commit to the marketplace listing and joint reference docs. High-level asks from our side:

- Confirm a recommended deployment pattern (hosted vs dedicated vs private) for our use case (multi-tenant inference with customer-managed keys).
- Run a short validation matrix: latency P95, throughput (100/500 reqs/min), KV cache correctness across sessions.
- Network and IP allowlist requirements for our VPC peering.

We're attaching an initial network diagram and will follow with details on sample payloads. Proposed window for validation: week of Aug 24–28 — we can be flexible.

Attachments: lumenforge_network_diagram.pdf (see also integrations@lumenforge.com)

Thanks,
Samir Patel
Head of Integrations, LumenForge
samir@lumenforge.com
+1 415-555-0198

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@lumenforge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 Aug 2026 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Redwood <> LumenForge connector — tech validation kickoff

Samir — thanks, team. Good summary. Quick responses and proposed next steps below.

Deployment recommendation
- For your multi-tenant mode with customer-managed keys we recommend Redwood Dedicated for the initial validation: predictable throughput and ability to pin a quantized variant for cost control. If customers require full network isolation we can run Redwood Private (VPC) in a follow-up pilot.

Validation matrix (short version)
- P95 generation latency at seq length 512: baseline vs quantized (q4_0)
- Throughput: 100 reqs/min (low) and 500 reqs/min (burst) using continuous batching; observe tail latency and drop rate.
- Correctness: session affinity + KV cache replay for multi-turn chat (3-turn sequences).
- Fallback check: validate automatic fallback to the smaller-medium model when capacity triggers.

Network / security questions for your team
- Can you confirm if the staging VPC will allow outbound to our public API endpoints, or do you require a private VPC peering?
- If peering is needed, please share the CIDR ranges and whether you will require mutual TLS for control-plane calls.

I've attached a compact reference architecture (redwood-reference-arch.yaml) that shows recommended routing, batchers, and a suggested canary strategy.

Proposed timeline
- Share any missing network details by EOD Tue Aug 24
- Redwood to provision a dedicated staging capacity and config by Aug 25–26
- Run validation tests Aug 27 (morning pacific)

Action items
- Samir: confirm network approach & attach CIDRs
- Elena/Integrations: upload sample payloads and rate profiles
- Marissa / Jonas / Mateo (Redwood): provision staging and test harness; we'll share the test-run playbook.

Attachments: redwood-reference-arch.yaml

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Principal Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.ai

Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Redwood <> LumenForge connector — tech validation kickoff

Quick note on the test plan and a few perf knobs to watch for.

Test matrix (detailed items we'll automate)
- Latency sweep: seq lengths [64, 256, 512], concurrency levels [1, 8, 32], record P50/P95/P99.
- Throughput burst: 100/min steady + 1 minute burst at 500/min to observe autoscaler and fallback behavior.
- KV cache: create 500 unique sessions, then replay cached prefixes to validate hits/misses and token-level alignment.
- Batching/Batch size: test continuous batching window=20–50ms vs disabled to quantify unit cost delta.
- Regression checks: smoke prompts to ensure deterministic structured outputs (JSON schema) after quantization.

Notes on model variants
- We'll run the nominal float32 model and q4_0 quantized model; expect a 20–40% latency reduction at cost of minor quality variance — include quality checks in the plan.

I've started the staging-test-plan.xlsx with the exact payloads and the Jenkins job names; will upload into the shared drive shortly.

Subject: Re: Kickoff: Redwood <> LumenForge connector — tech validation kickoff

Samir — thanks, team. Good summary. Quick responses and proposed next steps below.

Deployment recommendation
- For your multi-tenant mode with customer-managed keys we recommend Redwood Dedicated for the initial validation: predictable throughput and ability to pin a quantized variant for cost control. If customers require full network isolation we can run Redwood Private (VPC) in a follow-up pilot.

Validation matrix (short version)
- P95 generation latency at seq length 512: baseline vs quantized (q4_0)
- Throughput: 100 reqs/min (low) and 500 reqs/min (burst) using continuous batching; observe tail latency and drop rate.
- Correctness: session affinity + KV cache replay for multi-turn chat (3-turn sequences).
- Fallback check: validate automatic fallback to the smaller-medium model when capacity triggers.

Network / security questions for your team
- Can you confirm if the staging VPC will allow outbound to our public API endpoints, or do you require a private VPC peering?
- If peering is needed, please share the CIDR ranges and whether you will require mutual TLS for control-plane calls.

I've attached a compact reference architecture (redwood-reference-arch.yaml) that shows recommended routing, batchers, and a suggested canary strategy.

Proposed timeline
- Share any missing network details by EOD Tue Aug 24
- Redwood to provision a dedicated staging capacity and config by Aug 25–26
- Run validation tests Aug 27 (morning pacific)

Action items
- Samir: confirm network approach & attach CIDRs
- Elena/Integrations: upload sample payloads and rate profiles
- Marissa / Jonas / Mateo (Redwood): provision staging and test harness; we'll share the test-run playbook.

Attachments: redwood-reference-arch.yaml

Best,

Quick note on the test plan and a few perf knobs to watch for.

Test matrix (detailed items we'll automate)
- Latency sweep: seq lengths [64, 256, 512], concurrency levels [1, 8, 32], record P50/P95/P99.
- Throughput burst: 100/min steady + 1 minute burst at 500/min to observe autoscaler and fallback behavior.
- KV cache: create 500 unique sessions, then replay cached prefixes to validate hits/misses and token-level alignment.
- Batching/Batch size: test continuous batching window=20–50ms vs disabled to quantify unit cost delta.
- Regression checks: smoke prompts to ensure deterministic structured outputs (JSON schema) after quantization.

Notes on model variants
- We'll run the nominal float32 model and q4_0 quantized model; expect a 20–40% latency reduction at cost of minor quality variance — include quality checks in the plan.

I've started the staging-test-plan.xlsx with the exact payloads and the Jenkins job names; will upload into the shared drive shortly.

title: Reserved GPU island qualification — recap, roadmap, and open risks
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@aureliabio.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Aug 2026 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Reserved GPU island qualification — recap, roadmap, and open risks

Alice — thanks again for the time today. Below is a concise recap from our discovery call, the hypothesis for a dedicated/reserved pool that meets your needs, and the concrete next steps we discussed. I'm summarizing here so we have a single source-of-truth to hand to procurement and infra.

High-level goals / pain points
- PHI and model-data separation: Aurelia needs a VPC-isolated inference plane that satisfies internal audit for PHI handling (no cross-tenant mem sharing).
- Predictable throughput for batch + interactive: ~200–300 inference req/s at 8k tokens peak on daytime load (burst to ~600req/s during pipeline runs).
- Latency SLO: p95 < 250ms for short responses (<=128 tokens). Heavy tails during rounds are an issue today.
- Cost control: prefer predictable committed capacity with burst policy vs. unbounded on-demand.
- Operational handoffs: CS needs a simple runbook and alerting — lowest friction for on-call and escalation.

Redwood hypothesis (what we propose)
- Dedicated GPU island: reserved pool (8xA100-equivalent nodes) with tenant isolation and optional hardware-backed KMS.
- Isolation model: single-tenant runtime with kernel memory isolation + KV-cache per session; network egress locked to your VPC via PrivateLink.
- Scaling plan: base commit for 90% of steady-state; auto-burst to a shared burst pool (costed separately) for scheduled pipelines.
- SLA targets: baseline SLOs we recommend are 99.9% uptime and p95 latency budget of 250ms; we can discuss commercial SLA for p99 and credits if required.

Open risks / unknowns (items we need from Aurelia)
- Detailed request profile: percent of requests that are <=128 tokens vs. long-context -- will change batching/kv-cache config. Please share a 2-week traffic sample or a small synthetic load profile.
- Compliance artifacts: SOC2 report or auditor contact if you want us to map controls to the private deployment.
- Procurement window & billing: target start date you mentioned was late Sep. Confirm procurement approver and PO cadence.

Immediate next steps (owners & timelines)
- Mateo: deliver a draft Isolation + SLA one-pager and cost ranges (attached) — by Tue 8/24. [attachment: isolation-sla-draft.pdf]
- Ben: tuck in a preliminary ops MOP (runbook + alerting checklist) and scaling playbook — by Wed 8/25. [attachment: mop-outline.xlsx]
- Jordan: schedule a 60m deep-dive on architecture + networking with Aurelia infra (propose next Wed 8/26 or Thu 8/27).
- Alice / Aurelia: share traffic sample and procurement approver contact by EOD Fri 8/27.

If that looks right, we'll convert the draft SLA to a formal SOW and slot in engineering pre-work (network peering, KMS onboarding) as part of the Q3 delivery plan.

Thanks — appreciate the clarity today, and happy to tighten any of the items above.

— Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192
Redwood Inference

Attachments: isolation-sla-draft.pdf (PDF, 2 pages) ; mop-outline.xlsx (Excel, 1 sheet)


From: Alice Park <alice@aureliabio.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, procurement@aureliabio.com
Date: Fri, 20 Aug 2026 17:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Reserved GPU island qualification — recap, roadmap, and open risks

Thanks Mateo — this is helpful. Quick clarifications from our side: 

- The 200–300 req/s estimate is for our interactive endpoints; batch pipelines run nightly and can spike up to 800req/s for short windows. We'll provide a traffic sample this week.
- PHI: our security/compliance lead would like to confirm HSM/KMS integration and whether keys can be customer-managed. We have some baseline SOC2 artifacts but will need Redwood to map any gaps to our security questionnaire.
- SLA: we need p99 numbers for the baseline; commercial SLA is negotiable but p99 targets and an escalation path are critical. Can you propose p99 latency budgets and exact credit terms for missed SLOs?

I can do a deep-dive next Wed (8/26) at 10:00am PT if that works for your team; procurement will follow up separately on paperwork.

On the attachments — I saw the SLA draft and runbook stub, looks like a good starting point. Please include a short appendix on the burst policy and how preemption/fallback works when the reserved pool is saturated.

-Alice
Alice Park
Head of ML Infrastructure, Aurelia Bio
alice@aureliabio.com

On Fri, Aug 20, 2026 at 3:04 PM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> Alice — thanks again for the time today. Below is a concise recap from our discovery call...


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Aug 2026 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Reserved GPU island qualification — recap, roadmap, and open risks

Ben / Jordan — forwarding Alice's reply. Confirming the internal checklist before the Wed deep-dive (8/26 @ 10:00am PT):

- Ben: please prepare an ops appendix that covers "burst behavior" (how we route to shared pool, preemption, cold-cache effects) and a table of expected p95/p99 latencies for 1x/2x/4x node configs.
- Jordan: bring a short slide on the networking pattern (PrivateLink / VPC peering / egress controls) and the KMS options (customer-managed vs Redwood-managed). Also include an estimate for lead time on private endpoint provisioning.
- Mateo: I'll own converting the SLA appendix Alice requested into two concrete p99 proposals (conservative and aggressive) and a short credit schedule to present.

Procurement note: Alice said procurement will initiate PO paperwork after the architecture call. Let's have commercial ready with a draft SOW and two pricing bands (commit + burst) by EOD Mon 8/24 so we can move quickly. I've added the HubSpot link below.

HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/company/8421

Thanks — will sync Monday for a 30m prep.

— Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com

Subject: Re: Order form: Clauses disposition & signer matrix

Alex / team — thanks for sending. I reviewed Helio_OrderForm_v1_redlines.docx and annotated inline where we should (a) accept, (b) counter-propose, or (c) need legal policy sign-off. High-level notes:

- Billing / effective date: We can align the subscription start to production cutover provided the order form includes a firm activation confirmation process (we should add an activation acceptance clause and a clear invoice schedule). Proposed text: "Subscription start date will be the date of Redwood's written activation confirmation following Customer production cutover."

- Data retention / KV cache: Redwood's default is to persist KV cache for 180 days for features that enable cross-request context caching. For regulated customers we can offer a 90-day profile but need to triage with Product on operational impact. Suggested compromise language: "Redwood will configure KV cache retention per customer profile; for Helio, Redwood will treat specified indexes with a maximum retention of 90 days, subject to performance review and exception terms."

- SLA / credits: Helio is requesting a modified SLA window for customer-critical endpoints. If Helio requires a tighter SLA we will need an exceptions review (pricing impact likely). Please confirm which endpoints are in scope.

- Exhibits: Please add the Data Flow Diagram (data_flow_diagram.pdf) to Exhibit B and provide contact for security evidence (SOC 2 report).

I placed a handful of inline redlines in the doc where the language modifies core template indemnities and IP sections — those are negotiable but require Legal/NA approval. Karthik — if we need to offer a one-time credit to offset a shorter retention profile or SLA uplift, let's propose numbers before the Helio execs push on price.

I'll join the 10:30am sync if scheduled, but if Helio prefers async answers, I can produce a redline with our proposed compromise language by EOD.

Attachments referenced: annotated Helio_OrderForm_v1_redlines.docx (inline comments), data_flow_diagram.pdf

Best,
Kimberly Park

Redwood Inference
