title: Order Form commercial: invoice routing, min commit phasing, and tax handling
preview:
From: Elena Martinez <elena.martinez@catalysthealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@catalysthealth.com
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 09:17:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood Order Form — invoice split / net terms / VAT questions

Hi Mateo,

Thanks for the draft Order Form. A few procurement items we need to confirm before legal proceeds with countersignature:

- Invoicing: we need the ability to split invoicing across our US entity (Catalyst Health, Inc.) and our EU entity (Catalyst Health EMEA Ltd.) depending on usage by region. Can Redwood support per-region invoice recipient mapping? If so, how will invoices and credits be associated to the same deal?

- Net terms: Procurement prefers net30. Our finance team flagged potential need for net45 if multi-entity routing causes payment processing delays. Any flexibility here?

- Minimum commitment / phasing: We propose a 6‑month ramp: 40% initial commit, 30% month 3, 30% month 6. Can you confirm proration and pro‑rata invoice handling for partial-month activation?

- Taxes: We will provide VAT registration docs for EMEA; please confirm whether Redwood will gross-up or if Catalyst will self-account (reverse charge). Also need any VAT evidence required prior to invoice issuance.

I've attached the draft OF you sent back (we've annotated two small items) and our tax residency cert. Can you loop in legal and finance on Redwood's side so we can get a clear, consolidated response to procurement/legal?

Thanks — happy to hop on a 30‑minute call if that's easier.

Elena
Elena Martinez
Head of Procurement | Catalyst Health
elena.martinez@catalysthealth.com
+1 415 555 0192

Attachments: Redwood_OrderForm_Draft_v3.pdf (pdf, annotated), Catalyst_Tax_Residency_Cert.pdf (pdf)


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Martinez <elena.martinez@catalysthealth.com>
Cc: kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com, ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood Order Form — invoice split / net terms / VAT questions

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear list and the attachments. Looping in Kimberly (Legal) and Ben (Finance) to confirm specifics; I’ll summarize our preferred approach below so we can converge quickly:

1) Invoice routing by region: yes — Redwood supports per-region invoice routing. We can issue two invoices per month (US / EMEA) and allocate usage by billing tag. Credits/usage reconciliation will reference the same Deal ID and invoice numbers so accounting can match.

2) Net terms: standard is net30 on Hosted plans. For multi-entity routing where Catalyst requests split invoices, Finance sometimes prefers net45 to allow cross-entity processing. Ben — what would Redwood need to keep net30 vs request net45?

3) Min commit phasing & proration: we can model the 6‑month ramp you propose. Pro‑rata for partial months is supported; we will include a proration line on the first invoice for the activation month. If you need strict milestone-based invoicing instead of time-based, we can do that but it requires an amendment in the OF.

4) Taxes/VAT: we will accept Catalyst’s VAT cert for EMEA and can apply reverse charge (Catalyst self‑account) if you provide the cert prior to the first invoice for EMEA usage. Kimberly — are there any contract language changes we should request to capture VAT gross-up / reverse charge choice?

Next steps: Kimberly and Ben to propose two small redlines (one in Section 2.1 pricing schedule for invoice split, one in Section 7 tax clause for VAT handling). After internal redlines, I’ll send a DocuSign link with the revised OF.

If helpful, I can send a short one‑page diagram showing how monthly usage will split into two invoices and how credits flow between them.

Best,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Martinez <elena.martinez@catalysthealth.com>
Cc: ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 14:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood Order Form — invoice split / net terms / VAT questions

Thanks — quick legal take and suggested redline language (high level):

- Invoice routing / reconciliation (new clause suggestion): "Redwood may issue separate invoices attributable to usage recorded against different legal entities or geographic billing tags. All invoices shall reference the Agreement and Deal ID; sums due shall be aggregated for the purpose of applying Credits and service thresholds."

- Net terms: Redwood’s standard is net30. Given Catalyst’s ask for split invoicing, Finance preference is to retain net30 but require either (a) a 20% upfront activation deposit applied to the first invoice, or (b) net45 for split-entity arrangements. Suggested clause (commercial appendix): "Payment terms shall be net30. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Customer requests multi-entity split invoicing, Redwood may require an upfront deposit equal to 20% of the initial committed value, applied to the first invoice, in lieu of extending net45 terms."

- Taxes / VAT: Proposed contract language: "Taxes: Except as expressly provided herein, Customer shall be responsible for all taxes, duties or similar governmental assessments. For cross-border VAT, Customer shall provide valid VAT registration and residency evidence prior to the first invoice for which Customer seeks reverse charge treatment. If Customer does not provide such evidence in good time, Redwood reserves the right to charge VAT on the applicable invoices."

Ben — from Finance: if Catalyst can provide the VAT cert by June 8, we can keep net30 with the deposit option waived. If not, we will require net45 on any invoice where entity split requires intercompany settlement.

Elena — can you confirm whether Catalyst is comfortable with the deposit or able to get the VAT cert by next Monday? I’ve attached a short redline PDF (small markups) to speed review.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Commercial Contracts | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com

Attachment: Redwood_OrderForm_Redline_v3_legalnotes.pdf (pdf, redline comments)

On Wed, Jun 3, 2026 at 11:03 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena — thanks for the clear list and the attachments. Looping in Kimberly (Legal) and Ben (Finance) to confirm specifics; I’ll summarize our preferred approach below so we can converge quickly:
> ...

To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@catalysthealth.com

Subject: Re: Redwood Order Form — invoice split / net terms / VAT questions

- VAT cert: confirmed — I attached the Catalysts' VAT cert in my first note; our tax team will send a fully executed EU VAT registration extract by Friday 2026-06-06 EOD.

- Deposit: Finance prefers not to pay upfront if avoidable. If Redwood can keep net30 conditioned on receiving VAT evidence by 06/06, Catalyst accepts that. If evidence is delayed, we can accept a 20% deposit for activation instead of moving to net45.

- Invoicing: Catalyst prefers the per-region invoice approach you described. Please include the reconciliation line and Deal ID on both invoices; that will satisfy our accounting matching.

Next steps for Redwood: 1) incorporate the two redlines (invoice routing clause + tax clause) into the OF, 2) send revised OF via DocuSign, 3) confirm whether the deposit option will be enabled as a fallback in DocuSign (toggle box). title: ProcureCo vendor onboarding — buyer intake & bank details
preview:
From: Maya Singh <maya@procureco.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding kickoff — ProcureCo purchase process

Hi Mateo,

Thanks for the quick intro earlier this week. We're starting procurement for the Redwood contract and I wanted to kick off the vendor onboarding checklist so finance can set you up in our ERP.

Can you confirm what information you need from us to (1) create a vendor record, (2) accept purchase orders, and (3) send invoices? Typical items on our side are legal entity, tax docs (W‑9 or VAT), remit-to, preferred payment method (ACH or wire), and contact for SOC/audit questions.

We're targeting kickoff next week and would like to complete vendor setup within 7 business days if possible. I'll loop in finance on follow ups.

Thanks — please send whatever intake form you normally use and a quick note on PO requirements (PO number format, must-have fields on invoices, and invoice cadence).

Best,
Maya Singh
Senior Buyer, ProcureCo
maya@procureco.com
+1 (415) 555-0198


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Singh <maya@procureco.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding kickoff — ProcureCo purchase process

Hi Maya — thanks for the note and timeline. Attached is our standard buyer intake form (redwood_buyer_intake_form.pdf) — it covers the bulk of what you asked for. Below are the fields we need to complete vendor setup and the PO/invoice expectations.

Quick checklist (what we need from ProcureCo):
- Legal entity name exactly as registered (for contract & tax)
- Remit-to address (street, city, state/province, postal code, country)
- Tax identifier: W-9 (US EIN) or VAT registration number (EU/APAC) — we can accept a scanned PDF
- Preferred payment method: ACH (bank routing + acct last 4) or Wire (we will provide our wire details securely)
- Invoice contact and billing email for your AR team
- PO number rules: numeric + 6 digits preferred (e.g., 123456). If ProcureCo uses alphanumeric PO ids, please confirm prefix rules so we can map automatically.
- If ProcureCo requires COI (certificate of insurance) or additional tax forms, please attach or indicate in reply.

How Redwood processes invoices / PO matching:
- We can accept invoices via email to invoices@redwood.ai or via your AP portal (preferred: email for speed)
- Invoices must include: invoice number, invoice date, PO number, line items, subtotal, tax, total, remit-to instructions
- Payment terms: net 30 (default) — if ProcureCo requires different terms, let us know and we can include in the SOW/PO.

Attachments included: our intake form (fillable), a short PO requirements checklist, and a secure DocuSign link for ACH enrollment if you opt for direct deposit.

Next steps:
1) Complete the attached intake_form and return with the tax doc (W-9) and remit-to address.
2) Karthik (cc'd) will validate bank/payment setup once we have the signed ACH enrollment or wire instructions.
3) We will confirm vendor creation and send a sample invoice so you can test PO matching.

If 7 business days is your target, completing steps 1–2 by Weds will keep us on track. Happy to hop on a quick call if helpful.

To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
Cc: ProcureCo Finance <finance@procureco.com>
Date: Sun, 31 May 2026 11:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding kickoff — ProcureCo purchase process

Thanks — attached is our filled intake packet (see key fields below) and our W‑9 (ProcureCo_W9.pdf). We're comfortable with net 30 and the PO format you suggested. A couple of notes:

Key details (from attached form):
- Legal entity: ProcureCo, Inc.
- Remit-to: 220 Market St, Suite 1400, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA
- Tax ID: EIN 12-3456789 (W-9 attached)
- Preferred payment: Wire transfer initially; we'll set up ACH later. Please provide secure instructions for wire beneficiary details.
- Invoice contact: ar-team@procureco.com (CC: finance@procureco.com)
- PO format: numeric 7 digits; we will include leading zeros if needed (e.g., 0012345)

We will not share full bank details over email. Please send the DocuSign secure ACH/wire enrollment link you referenced and we will complete the form. If Redwood requires any supplemental tax or compliance docs (COI, sys attestation), let us know and we will escalate internally.

On the timeline: we should be able to return the secure wire enrollment within 48 hours after we receive the link. If that works, put us on the Friday onboarding target and we'll confirm once enrollment is submitted.

Thanks for the clear checklist. Let us know if you need anything else.

Best,
Maya Singh
Senior Buyer, ProcureCo
maya@procureco.com
Attachments: ProcureCo_W9.pdf
